title: Diagnosis: Anomalous Iteration Error in fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model_generalized
text: identity link, estimating equation LINEAR beta Weight function: W(t, beta) = V^{-1} B(t) (doesn’t depend beta) influence equation reduces : sum_i sum_j W(t_ij) * (Y_ij - B(t_ij)'beta) / lambda(t_ij) = 0 linear system can solved directly! original fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model solves analytically: BB::sane designed nonlinear systems Using linear system inefficient can fail large influence function values suggest system poorly conditioned
code: uncorrected.beta_hat <- (colSums(IT$term1) + colSums(IT$term2)) / length(IT$id) estimate <- as.vector(V_inverse %*% uncorrected.beta_hat)

what: Solution 1: Use Direct Solution for Identity Link (RECOMMENDED)
title: Diagnosis: Anomalous Iteration Error in fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model_generalized
text: identity link (regardless loss function), use analytic solution:
code: if (link == "identity") {     # Identity link has linear influence equation - solve directly     # Compute influence for all patients at beta = 0 (or any value - it's linear!)     influence_by_patient <- map(unique_ids, compute_influence_by_patient, beta = rep(0, ncol(base)))          # Sum influence terms     total_influence <- reduce(map(influence_by_patient, getElement, 'total'), `+`)          # Solve: V^{-1} * total_influence = 0     # => beta = solve(V, total_influence)     V <- GramMatrix(base)     solution_par <- solve(V, total_influence)          solution <- list(         par = solution_par,         convergence = 0,         message = "Analytic solution (identity link)"     ) } else {     # Non-identity links require iterative optimization     solution <- BB::sane(         par = rep(0, ncol(base)),         fn = influence,         control = BBsolve.control     ) }

what: Solution 2: Better Initial Values for BB::sane
title: Diagnosis: Anomalous Iteration Error in fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model_generalized
text: want keep using BB::sane links, use better starting values:
code: # Use the "naive" estimate as starting point naive_influence_by_patient <- map(unique_ids, compute_influence_by_patient, beta = rep(0, ncol(base))) naive_total <- reduce(map(naive_influence_by_patient, getElement, 'total'), `+`) V <- GramMatrix(base) initial_beta <- solve(V, naive_total)  solution <- BB::sane(     par = initial_beta,  # Better starting point     fn = influence,     control = BBsolve.control )

title: Fitting with Different Loss and Link Functions
text: framework let =aA=denote treatment arm, YY outcome variable, tt time variable. SensIAT package allows users specify different loss functions link functions fitting models data. flexibility enables users tailor modeling approach specific research questions data characteristics. Let μa(t)=E{Y(t)∣=}\mu_a(t) = E\{Y(t)\mid =\} denote population mean outcome time tt participants assigned treatment arm =aA=. mean function modeled $_a(t) = g{-1}((t)_a) = s((t)^_a) $ gg link function, 𝐁\mathbf{B} vector values basis function γa\gamma_a vector coefficients, distinct treatment arm. function s=g−1s = g^{-1} inverse link function. consider three loss functions use order fit coefficient γa\gamma_a: ℒ1(𝛄)=∫t=t1t2[g{μ(t)}−𝐁(t)′𝛄]2dt\mathcal{L}_1(\boldsymbol{\gamma}) = \int_{t=t_1}^{t_2} \left[ g\left\{\mu(t)\right\} - \boldsymbol{B}(t)^\prime \boldsymbol{\gamma}\right]^2 dt, squared error loss transformed space; ℒ2(𝛄)=∫t=t1t2[μ(t)−s{𝐁(t)′𝛄}]2dt\mathcal{L}_2(\boldsymbol{\gamma}) = \int_{t=t_1}^{t_2} \left[ \mu(t) - s\left\{\boldsymbol{B}(t)^\prime \boldsymbol{\gamma}\right\}\right]^2 dt, squared error loss original space; ℒ3(𝛄)=∫t=t1t2[b{𝐁(t)′𝛄}−μ(t)𝐁(t)′𝛄]dt\mathcal{L}_3(\boldsymbol{\gamma}) = \int_{t=t_1}^{t_2} \left[ b\{\boldsymbol{B}(t)^\prime \boldsymbol{\gamma}\} - \mu(t)\boldsymbol{B}(t)^\prime \boldsymbol{\gamma}\right] dt, quasi-likelihood loss, s(z)=∂b(z)∂zs(z) = \frac{\partial b(z)}{\partial z }. SensIAT package, implemented following link functions: Identity link: g(μ)=μg(\mu) = \mu, s(z)=zs(z) = z, Log link: g(μ)=log(μ)g(\mu) = \log(\mu), s(z)=exp(z)s(z) = \exp(z), Logit link: g(μ)=log(μ1−μ)g(\mu) = \log\left(\frac{\mu}{1-\mu}\right), s(z)=exp(z)1+exp(z)s(z) = \frac{\exp(z)}{1+\exp(z)}.

text: vignette compares integration methods computing term2 influence components fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model_generalized. isolate just term2 computation (numerical integration step) provide focused benchmarks without overhead full model fitting. Available methods: "fast" (default): Adaptive Simpson’s optimized closure-based integrand "original": Standard adaptive Simpson’s implementation "fixed_grid": Pre-computed expected values fixed grid composite Simpson’s "seeded_adaptive": Adaptive Simpson’s seeded pre-computed grid points evaluate across: Accuracy: Difference high-precision reference Speed: Direct term2 computation time Scalability: Performance across grid densities

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: use package’s example data fit required outcome model :
code:
# Load and prepare example data data_with_lags <- SensIAT_example_data |>   group_by(Subject_ID) |>   mutate(     ..prev_outcome.. = lag(Outcome, default = NA_real_, order_by = Time),     ..prev_time.. = lag(Time, default = 0, order_by = Time),     ..delta_time.. = Time - lag(Time, default = NA_real_, order_by = Time)   ) |>   ungroup()  cat("Data summary:
") cat("  Patients:", n_distinct(data_with_lags$Subject_ID), "
") cat("  Total observations:", nrow(data_with_lags), "
") # Fit outcome model (single-index with splines) outcome.model <- fit_SensIAT_single_index_fixed_coef_model(   Outcome ~ splines::ns(..prev_outcome.., df = 3) + ..delta_time.. - 1,   id = Subject_ID,   data = data_with_lags |> filter(Time > 0) )  # Imputation function for extrapolation impute_fn <- function(t, df) {   extrapolate_from_last_observation(     t, df, "Time",     slopes = c("..delta_time.." = 1)   ) }  # Marginal mean spline knots knots <- c(60, 260, 460)

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: benchmark_term2_methods() function directly compares term2 computation methods without repeatedly fitting full model. isolates integration performance care . Note: Results use precomputed benchmarks shipped package. regenerate: source(system.file("extdata", "generate_term2_benchmarks.R", package = "SensIAT"))
code: # Run isolated term2 benchmark benchmark_results <- benchmark_term2_methods(   data = data_with_lags,   id = Subject_ID,   time = data_with_lags$Time,   outcome.model = outcome.model,   knots = knots,   alpha = c(-0.05, 0, 0.05),   impute_data = impute_fn,   link = "identity",   methods = c("fast", "original", "fixed_grid", "seeded_adaptive"),   grid_sizes = c(50, 100, 200),   n_patients = 20,   n_iterations = 5,   reference_method = "fast" )

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: Term2 Computation Time Method
code: knitr::kable(   benchmark_results$timing |>     mutate(       mean_time = sprintf("%.4f", mean_time),       sd_time = sprintf("%.4f", sd_time),       relative_speed = sprintf("%.2f", relative_speed)     ),   caption = "Term2 Computation Time by Method",   col.names = c("Method", "Mean (s)", "SD (s)", "Min (s)", "Max (s)", "Relative") )

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: Accuracy measured fast method (adaptive Simpson’s): Accuracy vs Reference (fast method)
code: knitr::kable(   benchmark_results$accuracy |>     mutate(       max_abs_diff = sprintf("%.2e", max_abs_diff),       mean_abs_diff = sprintf("%.2e", mean_abs_diff),       rmse = sprintf("%.2e", rmse)     ) |>     select(method, max_abs_diff, rmse),   caption = "Accuracy vs Reference (fast method)",   col.names = c("Method", "Max |Diff|", "RMSE") )

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: Term2 computation time method
code: timing_df <- benchmark_results$timing |>   mutate(     method_type = case_when(       grepl("fixed_grid", method) ~ "Fixed Grid",       grepl("seeded", method) ~ "Seeded Adaptive",       TRUE ~ "Adaptive"     )   )  ggplot(timing_df, aes(x = reorder(method, mean_time), y = mean_time, fill = method_type)) +   geom_col() +   geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_time - sd_time, ymax = mean_time + sd_time), width = 0.2) +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Term2 Integration Performance",     subtitle = paste(benchmark_results$setup_info$n_patients, "patients,",                      benchmark_results$setup_info$n_alphas, "alpha values"),     x = NULL,     y = "Time (seconds)",     fill = "Method Type"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Term2 Integration Methods: Performance Comparison
text: accuracy scale grid density fixed-grid method? Fixed-grid accuracy vs grid density Speed-accuracy tradeoff
code: # Benchmark with varying grid sizes grid_benchmark <- benchmark_term2_methods(   data = data_with_lags,   id = Subject_ID,   time = data_with_lags$Time,   outcome.model = outcome.model,   knots = knots,   alpha = 0,   impute_data = impute_fn,   link = "identity",   methods = c("fast", "fixed_grid"),   grid_sizes = c(25, 50, 75, 100, 150, 200),   n_patients = 15,   n_iterations = 3,   reference_method = "fast" ) # Extract grid-specific results grid_accuracy <- grid_benchmark$accuracy |>   filter(grepl("fixed_grid", method)) |>   mutate(     grid_n = as.numeric(gsub("fixed_grid_", "", method))   )  grid_timing <- grid_benchmark$timing |>   filter(grepl("fixed_grid", method)) |>   mutate(     grid_n = as.numeric(gsub("fixed_grid_", "", method))   )  # Combine for plotting grid_combined <- left_join(grid_accuracy, grid_timing, by = c("method", "grid_n")) ggplot(grid_combined, aes(x = grid_n)) +   geom_line(aes(y = rmse), color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(aes(y = rmse), color = "steelblue", size = 3) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     title = "Fixed-Grid Accuracy vs Grid Density",     x = "Number of Grid Points",     y = "RMSE from Reference (log scale)"   ) +   theme_minimal() ggplot(grid_combined, aes(x = mean_time, y = rmse, label = grid_n)) +   geom_point(size = 4, color = "steelblue") +   geom_text(vjust = -1, hjust = 0.5) +   scale_y_log10() +   labs(     title = "Speed-Accuracy Tradeoff for Fixed-Grid Method",     subtitle = "Labels show number of grid points",     x = "Computation Time (seconds)",     y = "RMSE from Reference (log scale)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Extrapolate Variables from Last Observation — extrapolate_from_last_observation
text: function finds last observation data time less equal target_time, extrapolates variables using formula: $$x_{extrapolated} = x_{last} + (target\_time - time_{last}) \times slope$$ variables specified slopes, last observed value used (slope = 0).

title: Extrapolate Variables from Last Observation — extrapolate_from_last_observation

code: # Note: example takes approximately 30 seconds to run. # \donttest{ library(survival) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(splines) # Create followup data with lags # added variables `..prev_time..`, `..delta_time..` and `..prev_outcome..` # have special interpretations when computing the influence. data_with_lags <- SensIAT_example_data |>     dplyr::group_by(Subject_ID) |>     dplyr::mutate(         ..prev_outcome.. = dplyr::lag(Outcome, default = NA_real_, order_by = Time),         ..prev_time.. = dplyr::lag(Time, default = 0, order_by = Time),         ..delta_time.. = Time - dplyr::lag(.data$Time, default = NA_real_, order_by = Time)     )  # Create the observation time intensity model intensity.model <-     coxph(Surv(..prev_time.., Time, !is.na(Outcome)) ~ ..prev_outcome.. + strata(Visit),         data = data_with_lags |> dplyr::filter(.data$Time > 0)     )  # Create the observed outcome model outcome.model <-     fit_SensIAT_single_index_fixed_coef_model(         Outcome ~ ns(..prev_outcome.., df = 3) + ..delta_time.. - 1,         id = Subject_ID,         data = data_with_lags |> filter(Time > 0)     )  # Fit the marginal outcome model mm <- fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model(     data = data_with_lags,     id = Subject_ID,     alpha = c(-0.6, -0.3, 0, 0.3, 0.6),     knots = c(60, 260, 460),     intensity.model = intensity.model,     time.vars = c("..delta_time.."),     outcome.model = outcome.model ) # }

title: Fit the marginal mean model for generalize outcomes. — fit_SensIAT_marginal_mean_model_generalized
text: function offers four integration methods different performance/accuracy tradeoffs: Adaptive Methods (fast, original): Use adaptive Simpson's quadrature automatic subdivision Best accuracy irregular integrands fast method uses optimized closure-based integrand construction Fixed-Grid Method (fixed_grid): Pre-computes expected values fixed grid points (per alpha) Uses composite Simpson's rule integration 2-5x faster optimizing beta (multiple iterations) Best smooth integrands sufficient grid density Seeded Adaptive Method (seeded_adaptive): Combines pre-computation adaptive refinement Starts pre-computed grid, subdivides needed Good balance speed accuracy Gauss-Legendre Method (gauss_legendre): Uses Gauss-Legendre quadrature via statmod::gauss.quad() Highly accurate smooth integrands fewer evaluation points Exact polynomials degree 2n-1 using n points Requires statmod package

text: Term1 Optimizations (alpha-independent): Y-scaled observations pre-computed Patient index mappings cached O(1) lookups Identity link: weights pre-computed (depend beta alpha) Single-index models: global PMF constants extracted Term2 Optimizations: Integration grids pre-computed (alpha-independent) Basis evaluations grid points pre-computed Expected values computed per alpha (grid methods) Per-patient caching expected values Weight functions use numerically stable exp(-μ) multiplication Weight functions use numerically stable exp(-μ) multiplication Fast method uses closure-based integration reduced allocations

text: n_subjects Number subjects simulate. End Maximum follow-time. intensity_coef Coefficient effect previous outcome observation intensity.
